title: How DocGo (DCGO) Is Rewriting Its Story As Targets Fall And Transport Gains Focus
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

DocGo’s updated fair value estimate now sits at US$2.10, trimmed from US$2.20, signaling a slightly more cautious price target in the latest model. That move lines up with mixed Street commentary, where bullish views around the transport segment compete with concern over lower targets and recent Q4 variability. Read on to see how this shifting narrative fits with the new guidance, recent buybacks, and what to watch next as sentiment continues to evolve.

Stay updated as the Fair Value for DocGo shifts by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on DocGo.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Stifel keeps a Buy rating on DocGo even after lowering its price target to US$2.50 from US$4, indicating that the firm still sees upside potential at current levels. Stifel points to improving growth in the transport segment and estimates that transportation could potentially generate US$15m to US$20m of 2026 EBITDA pre overhead, and argues this unit alone could be worth more than the current share price.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Stifel describes Q4 results as mixed, which keeps execution in focus and may limit how much confidence some investors place on the updated outlook. Canaccord has lowered its price target on DocGo by US$0.50, adding to a pattern of reduced targets that reflects more cautious assumptions in recent models.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NasdaqCM:DCGO 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for DocGo. See which could impact your investment.

How This Changes the Fair Value For DocGo

Fair value is now set at US$2.10, reduced from US$2.20. Revenue growth assumptions now reflect a 56.12% decline, compared with a 38.69% decline previously. Net profit margin is now modeled at 5.12%, adjusted from 5.16%. The future P/E multiple assumption is now 15.38x, trimmed from 15.90x. The discount rate remains unchanged at 6.98%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company's real world story to a financial forecast and fair value, so you can see how contracts, margins, and growth plans feed into the numbers. They also refresh as new earnings, guidance, or news come through, keeping the thesis up to date.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on DocGo to stay up to date on:

How DocGo aims to build its mobile health and at home care programs with major payers and public health systems. The role of logistics and AI driven tools in improving efficiency, clinician utilization, and the transport segment's EBITDA potential. Key risks around the loss of migrant related government contracts, thin transport margins, and reliance on large institutional and government customers.
